folio.label: 181v
folio.l: For whan fortune , wil a man forsake
She beretú a wey his regne , & his richesse
And eke hise frendes , bothe more & lesse
For what man æ hatú frendes , thurgú fortune
Mishap~ , wyl make hem enemys , I gesse
This ¸uäbe is ful sotú , & ful comune
¢ Cenobia , of Palymerie Quene
As wryten îsiens , in here noblesse
So worthy was in armes , & so kene
That no wigút passed hire , in hardynesse
Ne in lynage , ne i~i~~ other gentillesse
Of kynges blod of Perce , is she descended
I sey nat , æ she had , moost fairestnesse
But of hire shap~ , she mygút nat ben amended
¢ From hire childhode , I fynde æ she fledde
Office of wo~men , and to wode she went
And many a wylde hertes blood , she shedde
Witú arwes brode , æ she to he~ sente
She was so swift , æ she a noon he~ hente
And whan æ she was eldre , she wolde kille
Leones , lepardes , and Beres al to rente
And in hire armes , welde he~ at hire wille
¢ She durste , wilde Bestes dennes , seke
And rennen in the Mountaignes , al the nygút
And slepe vnder the Bussú , & she coude eke
Wrastlen by verrey force , and verry mygút
W any ©onge man , were he neuäe so wygút
There mygút no thyngö , in hire armes stonde
She kept hire maydenhode , from euäy wigút
To no man deigned hire , for to be bonde
¢ But atte laste , hire frendes han hire maried
To Onedake , a Pénce of that contree
Al were it so , æ she he~ longe taried
And ©e shuln vnderstonde , how that he
Hadde suche fantasies , as hadde she
But nathelees , whan they were knytte in fere
They lyued in ioye , and in felicitee
For ecú of he~ had other , lief & dere
¢ Saue oo thyngö , æ she wolde neuäe assent
By no wey , æ he shulde by hire lye
But oones , for it was hire pleyn entent
To haue a child , the world to multiplie
And also sone , as æ she mygút espie
That she was nat witú childe , w æat dede
